channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Tinubu welcomes former Sokoto gov candidate to APC, urges unity
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu welcomes Sa’idu Umar Ubandoma back to APC, urging unity and support. Read more about Ubandoma’s return and integration into the party.
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-welcomes-former-sokoto-gov-candidate-to-apc-urges-unity/

item.title: UK police hunt man suspected of killing wife, two daughters, fleeing to Zimbabwe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
UK police are hunting Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, suspected of murdering his wife and two daughters, after he fled to Zimbabwe. Read more on the urgent inter
Read More: https://punchng.com/uk-police-hunt-man-suspected-of-killing-wife-two-daughters-fleeing-to-zimbabwe/

item.title: NUPRC hails ExxonMobil’s $1bn deepwater investment, awards 19 oil licences
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NUPRC welcomes ExxonMobil’s $1bn investment in Nigeria’s Usan deepwater project, set to boost crude oil production by 40,000 bpd and revive drilling activi
Read More: https://punchng.com/nuprc-hails-exxonmobils-1bn-deepwater-investment-awards-19-oil-licences/

item.title: Allegations rock Nigerian Embassy over human trafficking scams in Mali
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description: The House of Representatives has intensified efforts to strengthen Nigeria’s response to human trafficking, declaring that repatriating trafficked Nigerians from Mali and other West African countries is no longer enough unless backed by comprehensive rehabilitation and reintegration programmes

item.title: No aggrieved aspirant would be left behind, Oyo APC chieftains assure members
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo APC chieftains, Senator Olufemi Lanlehin and Prof. Taoreed Adedoja, assure members that all grievances will be addressed in the ongoing Oyo APC reconci
Read More: https://punchng.com/no-aggrieved-aspirant-would-be-left-behind-oyo-apc-chieftains-assure-members/

item.title: Opposition will cry ‘rigging’ when Tinubu wins 2027 election, Akpabio predicts
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Senate President Godswill Akpabio predicts opposition parties will cry ‘rigging’ when President Bola Tinubu wins the 2027 election. Read more on his remark
Read More: https://punchng.com/opposition-will-cry-rigging-when-tinubu-wins-2027-election-akpabio-predicts/

item.title: PFIPC: Reps investigate ‘fake’ agency’s ₦1.3bn budget claims

item.description:
The House of Representatives is investigating the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC) over N1.3bn budget claims, alleging it’s a ‘fak

item.title: IMF Retains Nigeria’s 2026 Economic Growth Outlook At 4.1%
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Says higher prices for essentials will further exacerbate poverty, food insecurity The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has maintained its growth forecasts for Nigeria’s economy in 2026 and 2027 at 4.1 per cent and 4.3 percent respectively. The Fund, which stated this in its latest World Eco

item.title: FG Signs Hosting Agreement For 2027 African School Games
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: The Federal government has signed a hosting agreement for the 2027 African School Games with the Association of National Olympic Committees of Africa (ANOCA). The signing ceremony, which took place in Abuja on Wednesday, marked a significant step in Nigeria’s quest to stage a successful second

item.title: 2027: Presidency dismisses Peter Obi’s safety concerns, says claims false
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Presidency has dismissed claims by National Democratic Coalition (NDC) leader and former Labour Party presidential candidate, Peter Obi, that his safety is under threat ahead of the 2027 general election, describing the allegations as false and misleading. The post 2027: Presidency dismisses Pet

item.title: Kwara Poly Grants Full Scholarship To Physically Challenged Student
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The intervention, which includes a living stipend, has been described as a life-changing gesture that restores her hope of completing her academic programme. The post Kwara Poly Grants Full Scholarship To Physically Challenged Student appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Osun AAC guber candidate calls for sweeping reforms in Nigeria’s political system
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The governorship candidate of the African Action Congress, AAC, in the forthcoming Osun State governorship election, Olajide Esan, has called for sweeping reforms to Nigeria’s political system, saying the current structure discourages competent citizens from seeking public office. Esan made th

item.title: Investors haven’t been this bullish on the dollar in a decade. How the buck can keep climbing.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: A crowded bet on a stronger U.S. dollar may depend on whether Wednesday’s jump in oil prices proves lasting, as renewed Middle East tensions revive inflation concerns and bolster expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to keep policy tight.

item.title: Dollar to Naira Exchange Rate Today, July 78, 2026: Nigerian Naira extends depreciation
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Naira extended its depreciation against the dollar on Wednesday at the official foreign exchange market. Central Bank of Nigeria’s data showed that the Naira dropped to N1,379.07 per dollar on Wednesday, down from N1,375.75 traded on Tuesday. This means that on a day-to-day basis, the Nige

item.title: Trader Joe's, Lowe's Embrace Mini Mania To Boost Sales
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Mini mania is sweeping through US retailers as shoppers on a budget love colorful, charming, tiny versions of common products. Bloomberg’s Sunny Kim has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Group Urges FG To Probe Alleged Threats To Peter Obi’s Life
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The NDC-Peter Obi Support Network (NDC-POSN) has called on the Federal Government to investigate allegations by the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, that his life may be under threat. In a statement issued in Abuja by the group spokesperson, Mrs Mercy Akums

item.title: ‘Obi inventing stories to attract undue sympathy’ – Presidency
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Presidency has dismissed allegations by former Anambra State governor and Labour Party presidential candidate, Peter Obi, that he is being targeted by the Federal Government, describing the claims as false and misleading. The post ‘Obi inventing stories to attract undue sympathy’ &#8

item.title: Rashford dilemma for Man Utd - Thursday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Manchester United are in a quandary over Marcus Rashford's future, Arsenal and Tottenham eye Antonio Nusa, Barcelona shortlist Bournemouth's Eli Junior Kroupi, plus more.

item.title: Transfer: Nigerian defender seals move to FK Austria Wien
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Austrian Bundesliga club FK Austria Wien have completed the signing of Nigerian defender Ibrahim Buhari, DAILY POST reports. The Violets signed Buhari from Swedish first division side IF Elfsborg. Buhari put pen to paper on a three-year contract with the 24-time Bundesliga champions. The 24-year-old

item.title: Army troops intercept woman, child allegedly heading to terrorist enclave in Borno
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the 26 Task Force Brigade under Operation HADIN KAI have intercepted a 20-year-old woman and her two-year-old child while she was allegedly attempting to make her way into a terrorist-held enclave near the Mandara Mountains in Borno State. This was made know by a security expert, Zagazola

item.title: US launches new wave of strikes against Iran after promising to 'hit them hard'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Explosions have been reported by Iranian state media in parts of the country's south.

item.title: Reps Constitute Panel To Probe PFIPC
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives on Wednesday constituted an ad hoc committee led by the Chairman of its Committee on Navy, Hon. Yusuf Gagdi (APC, Plateau), to investigate the legality or otherwise of the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC). The resolution followed the adoption o

item.description: The House of Representatives on Wednesday received President Bola Tinubu's bill seeking to strengthen the administration and governance of... The post Tinubu seeks Reps’ approval of bill seeking to strengthen secondary school education appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.description: The awards ceremony is scheduled for 14 September 2026. The post The Pitt, Hacks, Widow’s Bay lead Emmy Awards nominations 2026 (FULL LIST) appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Chelsea sign 19-yr-old Geovany Quenda from Sporting Lisbon
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Chelsea have confirmed the signing of highly-rated Portuguese winger Geovany Quenda from Sporting Lisbon, with the 19-year-old putting pen to paper on a long-term contract that runs until 2034. The post Chelsea sign 19-yr-old Geovany Quenda from Sporting Lisbon appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Wike doesn’t sleep until the job is done, Tinubu hails minister
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu praises FCT Minister Nyesom Wike for his tireless work ethic and commitment to delivering projects, highlighting Abuja’s transformation.

item.title: Transfer: Man Utd agree £50m deal with Chelsea for Andrey Santos
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Manchester United have struck a deal with Chelsea to sign Andrey Santos for £50million. Sky Sports reports that United will pay a base fee of £48 million plus an additional £2million in bonuses. Chelsea will keep a 10% share if Santos is sold in the future. Santos moved to Chelsea from Vasco da Gama

item.title: Monaco open to offers for Balogun after heroic World Cup
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Monaco's general manager Thiago Scuro said on Wednesday the Ligue 1 club were willing to sell US striker Folarin Balogun and France winger Maghnes Akliouche. The post Monaco open to offers for Balogun after heroic World Cup appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: CAC begins enforcement of company letter rules August 1
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has announced that it will begin enforcing statutory requirements on the contents of company business letters from August 1, 2026, warning that defaulting companies will face sanctions. The commission disclosed this in a public notice signed by its management a

item.title: Nigerian Navy recovers 39,500 litres of stolen crude oil in Rivers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: In order to discourage criminal activities involving illegal crude oil, the Nigerian Navy has intensified its crackdown on crude oil theft under Operation Delta Sentinel, recovering 39,500 litres of illegal crude oil from storage facilities and destroying the facilities in Bonny Local Government Are

item.description: The IMF has cut its global growth forecast for the second time this year, flagging "uncertainty and risks" brought on by the war in Iran. item.description: Bloomberg's Michelle Davis joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals" to discuss what's top of mind in media M&A as moguls gather in Sun Valley for Allen & Co.'s annual conference. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Earlier this week, a picture that seemed to show Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell covered in tubes in a hospital bed in a state of extreme distress. It turned out to be an AI-generated fake.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigeria LNG Limited (NLNG) has identified a significant future gas supply gap, saying the shortfall presents a major investment opportunity to unlock the country’s vast gas resources and strengthen both domestic supply and exports. This was made known during a panel session titled “Reali

item.title: U-17 WWCQ: Flamingos Head To Lome For Benin Return Leg
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria’s Flamingos will depart Lagos for Lomé, Togo on Thursday for the decisive second leg of their final qualifying fixture against Benin Republic in the race for a place at the 2026 FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup, Completesports.com reports. Akeem Busari’s side will travel from Ik

item.title: This chart shows stamp prices over the years as USPS hikes postage again this weekend
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The new rate will go into effect on July 12, and will be the eighth price increase over the last five calendar years

item.title: Nigeria probes fictitious 'presidential' agency
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In tonight's edition, Nigeria's government investigates how a fictitious agency wielding forged appointment letters from the president accessed government funds. Also, Nigeria's 36 states are soon to ratify a major police reform that would allow them to set up their own forces. And in Mauritania cl

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is back under ‘full-conflict conditions’ — and energy markets are scrambling
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices surge as U.S. and Iran trade strikes; but even without further disruption, volatility appears here to stay for energy markets

item.title: Global cancer cases may hit 35m by 2050 – WHO
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: WHO on Wednesday said that cancer causes more than 26,000 deaths daily, with 20.6 million new cases yearly worldwide.

item.title: CAC to sanction companies over non-compliance with business letter rules
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The commission said enforcement would take effect from 1 August, adding that non-compliant customers and companies would be subject to sanctions prescribed by law. The post CAC to sanction companies over non-compliance with business letter rules appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Unidentified gunmen kill Benue university lecturer at residence
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A lecturer in the Department of History at Moses Orshio Adasu University, Makurdi (MOAUM), Prof Gabriel Nyityo, was reportedly killed by unidentified gunmen in the early hours of Wednesday at his residence in Makurdi. Reports indicate that the assailants attacked the professor while he was asleep. H

item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force North West, Operation FANSAN YAMMA, have foiled coordinated attacks by suspected bandits, neutralised a notorious commander and disrupted a planned large-scale assault through precision airstrikes in separate operations in Kaura Namoda Local Government Area of Zamfara

item.description: …Urges Members To Shun Inflammatory Comments The Chairman of the Kwara State All Progressives Congress (APC) Reconciliation Committee, retired Col. AbdulWahab Ademola Lawal, has appealed to aggrieved members of the party to embrace dialogue and reconciliation following disagreements arising fr

item.title: Economic Costs For US, Iran Too Great for 'All-Out War,' Says CSIS's Will Todman
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Will Todman, chief of staff of the Geopolitics and Foreign Policy Department and senior fellow of the Middle East Program at CSIS, said that a low level conflict with outbursts from both the US and Iran is more likely than an 'all-out' war because the economic costs to both countries are too great.

item.title: Oil Jumps to Two Week High on US-Iran Jitters | Closing Bell
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Bailey Lipschultz. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Gunmen Abduct 3 Varsity Students In Nasarawa, Police Rescue 1
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Gunmen have abducted three students of the Federal University of Lafia in Nasarawa State, with the police rescuing one of the victims while intensifying efforts to secure the release of the remaining two. The students were kidnapped at about 9:20 p.m. on Monday at Living Seed Lodge, an off-campus ho

item.dc:creator: The Sun

Christopher Musa (retd.), has directed security personnel fighting terrorism and banditry to engage armed criminals immediately without waiting for further instructions, warning that any operative who hesitates in the line of duty would be regarded as aiding the e

item.title: Dangote Refinery reduced petrol price by N175 per litre
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Dangote Refinery reduced the premium motor spirit ex-depot price by N175 per litre in the last three weeks, DAILY POST reports. Checks by DAILY POST on Wednesday showed that Dangote Refinery had adjusted fuel price at least a fourth time to N1,075 per litre from N1,250 on June 16, 2026. This means t

item.title: Egypt FA: Can't be silent on Argentina referee cal...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The Egyptian FA said it "cannot remain silent regarding the refereeing decisions" following the team's round-of-16 loss to Argentina at the World Cup.

item.title: Sources: Portugal eye Jorge Jesus for head coach
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Portugal's Football Federation hope to announce Roberto Martinez's successor by the end of the week, with veteran coach Jorge Jesus the leading candidate to take over the national team.

item.title: EU lawmakers eye investigating FIFA's Infantino
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Dozens of European lawmakers are gathering support to launch an investigation in the European Parliament of FIFA boss Gianni Infantino over his involvement in the decision to permit U.S. striker Folarin Balogun to play despite an earlier red card.

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: We're into the quarterfinals at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and the race for the Golden Boot -- given to the tournament's top goal scorer -- is red-hot.

item.title: Lagos Assembly advances bill seeking to ban street begging
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The proposed legislation, debated during plenary, seeks to curb street begging and establish a legal framework for addressing a persistent social problem affecting security, sanitation and the image of Nigeria's commercial capital. item.description: After he was discovered to still be alive, the 18-month-old was flown to another hospital and survived.

item.title: Senate Passes e-Health Bill For Second Reading To Digitise Hospitals, Boost Rural Care
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Senate, on Wednesday, passed for second reading a bill seeking to establish a legal and regulatory framework for electronic health services in Nigeria, with a focus on digitising hospitals and expanding access in rural and underserved areas. The e-Health Bill, 2026 (SB. 758), sponsored by Deputy

item.title: US summer travel slumps under rising fares and fuel expenses
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US summer trips decline as 45 percent of Americans skip holidays due to soaring airfares and car travel expenses.

item.title: Aviation Unions Push For Privatisation Of NAMA To Modernise Air Navigation
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Joint Action Committee of Aviation unions has called for the commercialisation or privatisation of the Nigerian Airspace Management Agency (NAMA) saying the move is necessary to modernise air navigation infrastructure and improve safety. In a joint statement issued on Wednesday, the unions under

item.title: Show HN: Hnwork.app – UI for Who is hiring posts
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Hey HN, I built a UI on top of the "Who is hiring" posts. Take a look at https://hnwork.app!One of the downsides of unstructured text posts is the readability due to it being free-form and having little to no format. While there are other tools that have been built over the years to make perusing Wh

item.title: Get a $30 credit when you reserve Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy phones
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Even though they haven’t been officially announced yet, Samsung is giving you a chance to save some cash when you preorder what we’re expecting to be the brand’s updated Galaxy Z Fold phones. The next Galaxy Unpacked event will take place on July 22nd, 2026, and features the tagline “A new shape unf

item.title: ‘Other political parties are sleeping’ – Akpabio predicts Tinubu’s victory in 2027
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The President of the Senate, Senator Godswill Akpabio has said that the City Boy Movement is posied to ensure maximum victory The post ‘Other political parties are sleeping’ – Akpabio predicts Tinubu’s victory in 2027 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Trump ordered to pay E Jean Carroll $5.8m after failed appeal
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Trump ordered to pay $5.8m after the US Supreme Court rejected his appeal in the Carroll defamation and abuse case.

item.title: Actresses Of Nigerian-Descent Ayo Edebiri, Joy Sunday Earn 2026 Emmy Nominations

item.description: Their recognition continues a rise in Nigerian representation at the Emmys in recent years. The post Actresses Of Nigerian-Descent Ayo Edebiri, Joy Sunday Earn 2026 Emmy Nominations appeared first on Channels Television.

item.description: Fenerbahce are growing increasingly confident of signing Mason Greenwood from Marseille.

item.title: APC first-time governors declare support for Tinubu
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Fifteen first-term governors declare support for Tinubu’s second-term bid, backing policy continuity, reforms and stronger federal-state cooperation. The post APC first-time governors declare support for Tinubu appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Senate advances bill to jail fake drug offenders for 15 years
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday moved to strengthen Nigeria’s fight against counterfeit medicines and unsafe food products by passing for second read more Senate advances bill to jail fake drug offenders for 15 years

item.title: Ronaldo’s ex-coach to become new Portugal manager
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Jorge Jesus is set to become the new head coach of the Portugal national football team following the departure of Roberto Martinez. The post Ronaldo’s ex-coach to become new Portugal manager appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has dismissed President Bola Tinubu’s directive to the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences read more Atiku rejects Tinubu’s ICPC probe, seeks independent inquiry

item.title: Osigwe warns against politicising NBA ahead of 2027 election
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA), Afam Osigwe (SAN), has cautioned lawyers against allowing political interests to influence the association's affairs ahead of the 2027 general election, warning that doing so would undermine the integrity of the legal profession. The post Osigwe warns
